     1|/*
     2|Package leaves is pure Go implemetation of prediction part for GBRT (Gradient
     3|Boosting Regression Trees) models from popular frameworks.
     4|
     5|Recommended API (v1.0+ inference, v4.3 defaults)
     6|
     7|	m, err := leaves.LoadFromFile("model.json", leaves.DefaultLoadOptions())
     8|	// DefaultLoadOptions enables AutoTransform (probabilities for binary:logistic, etc.)
     9|	p, err := m.PredictSingle(features, 0)
    10|
    11|Training (v2.x): leaves.NewLearner / train.NewLearner, Fit, Save, ResumeFit.
    12|Data: leaves.LoadDataAuto / data.FromFileAuto (content sniffing).
    13|Convenience: leaves.NewLearnerFromModelAndData (infer objective from reference JSON).
    14|See README §格式自动识别 and 演进计划.md v4.3.
    15|
    16|Legacy API: LGEnsembleFromFile / XGEnsembleFromFile still work and delegate to
    17|model.Ensemble internally via ensemble_delegate.go.
    18|
    19|General
    20|All loaded models exibit the same interface from `Ensemble struct`. One can
    21|use method `Name` to get string representation of model origin. Possible name
    22|values are "lightgbm.gbdt", "lightgbm.rf", "xgboost.gbtree", "xgboost.gblinear", etc.
    23|
    24|# LightGBM model
    25|
    26|Example: binary classification
    27|
    28|build_breast_cancer_model.py:
    29|
    30|	import lightgbm as lgb
    31|	import numpy as np
    32|	from sklearn import datasets
    33|	from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
    34|
    35|	X, y = datasets.load_breast_cancer(return_X_y=True)
    36|	X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(X, y, test_size=0.2, random_state=0)
    37|
    38|	n_estimators = 30
    39|	d_train = lgb.Dataset(X_train, label=y_train)
    40|	params = {
    41|	    'boosting_type': 'gbdt',
    42|	    'objective': 'binary',
    43|	}
    44|	clf = lgb.train(params, d_train, n_estimators)
    45|	y_pred = clf.predict(X_test)
    46|	y_pred_raw = clf.predict(X_test, raw_score=True)
    47|
    48|	clf.save_model('lg_breast_cancer.model')  # save the model in txt format
    49|	np.savetxt('lg_breast_cancer_true_predictions.txt', y_pred)
    50|	np.savetxt('lg_breast_cancer_true_predictions_raw.txt', y_pred_raw)
    51|	np.savetxt('breast_cancer_test.tsv', X_test, delimiter='\t')

    56|

    64|
    65|	func main() {

    68|		if err != nil {
    69|			panic(err)

    73|		model, err := leaves.LGEnsembleFromFile("lg_breast_cancer.model", true)
    74|		if err != nil {

    77|		fmt.Printf("Name: %s\n", model.Name())
    78|		fmt.Printf("NFeatures: %d\n", model.NFeatures())

    80|		fmt.Printf("NEstimators: %d\n", model.NEstimators())
    81|		fmt.Printf("Transformation: %s\n", model.Transformation().Name())
    82|
    83|		// loading true predictions as DenseMat
    84|		truePredictions, err := mat.DenseMatFromCsvFile("lg_breast_cancer_true_predictions.txt", 0, false, "\t", 0.0)
    85|		if err != nil {

    87|		}
    88|		truePredictionsRaw, err := mat.DenseMatFromCsvFile("lg_breast_cancer_true_predictions_raw.txt", 0, false, "\t", 0.0)
    89|		if err != nil {

    91|		}
    92|

    94|		predictions := make([]float64, test.Rows*model.NOutputGroups())
    95|		// do predictions
    96|		model.PredictDense(test.Values, test.Rows, test.Cols, predictions, 0, 1)
    97|		// compare results
    98|		const tolerance = 1e-6
    99|		if err := util.AlmostEqualFloat64Slices(truePredictions.Values, predictions, tolerance); err != nil {
   100|			panic(fmt.Errorf("different predictions: %s", err.Error()))
   101|		}
   102|
   103|		// compare raw predictions (before transformation function)
   104|		rawModel := model.EnsembleWithRawPredictions()

   186|		// preallocate slice to store model predictions
   187|		predictions := make([]float64, csr.Rows()*model.NOutputGroups())
   188|		// do predictions
   189|		model.PredictCSR(csr.RowHeaders, csr.ColIndexes, csr.Values, predictions, 0, 1)
   190|		// compare results
   191|		const tolerance = 1e-6
   192|		// compare results. Count number of mismatched values beacase of floating point
   193|		// tolerances in decision rule
   194|		mismatch, err := util.NumMismatchedFloat64Slices(truePredictions.Values, predictions, tolerance)
   195|		if err != nil {
   196|			panic(err)
   197|		}
   198|		if mismatch > 2 {
   199|			panic(fmt.Errorf("mismatched more than %d predictions", mismatch))
   200|		}
   201|		fmt.Printf("Predictions the same! (mismatch = %d)\n", mismatch)
   202|	}
   203|
   204|Output:
   205|
   206|	Name: xgboost.gbtree
   207|	NFeatures: 4
   208|	NOutputGroups: 3
   209|	NEstimators: 5
   210|	Predictions the same! (mismatch = 0)
   211|
   212|# Notes on XGBoost DART support
   213|
   214|Please note that one must not provide nEstimators = 0 when predict with DART models from xgboost. For more details see xgboost's documentation.
   215|
   216|# Notes on LightGBM DART support
   217|
   218|Models trained with 'boosting_type': 'dart' options can be loaded with func `leaves.LGEnsembleFromFile`.
   219|But the name of the model (given by `Name()` method) will be 'lightgbm.gbdt', because LightGBM model format doesn't distinguish 'gbdt' and 'dart' models.
   220|*/

    11|## 引言
    12|
    13|_leaves_ 者，**纯 Go** 所写之 GBRT（梯度提升回归树）**训练与推理**库也。支持加载 XGBoost / LightGBM / scikit-learn 模型，亦可自训练（hist/exact、rank、survival、tweedie 等），无需 C 语言 API 绑定。
    14|
    15|**推荐入口**：`leaves.LoadFromFile`（推理，默认按 objective 自动变换）、`leaves.LoadDataAuto` / `leaves.NewLearner`（训练）。遗留 API `LGEnsembleFromFile` / `XGEnsembleFromFile` 仍可用。
    16|
    17|## 功能
    18|
    19|  * 通用功能：
    20|    * 支持批量并行预测
    21|    * 支持 sigmoid 与 softmax 变换函数；`DefaultLoadOptions()` 默认 **AutoTransform**（按 objective 自动启用）
    22|    * 支持提取决策树之叶节点索引
    23|    * **训练数据内容嗅探**（libsvm / 排序 TSV / CSV·TSV，扩展名仅作回退）
    24|  * 支持 LightGBM（[代码仓库](https://github.com/Microsoft/LightGBM)）模型：
    25|    * 可从 `text` 格式及 `JSON` 格式读入模型
    26|    * 支持 `gbdt`、`rf`（随机森林）与 `dart` 模型
    27|    * 支持多分类预测
    28|    * 针对类别特征有额外优化（如 _独热_ 决策规则）
    29|    * 针对纯预测场景有额外优化
    30|  * 支持 XGBoost（[代码仓库](https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost)）模型：

    37|    * 目标：regression、binary/multi、gamma/poisson、tweedie、survival:cox/aft、rank

    95|### 格式自动识别（v4.3）
    96|
    97|**推理**：`io.LoadFromFile` / `io.DetectFormat` 按扩展名与文件头识别模型；经典 XGB 二进制无魔数时用 header 探测；`.pkl`/`.joblib` → sklearn；数值表误作 `.txt` 模型时明确报错。
    98|
    99|**训练**：`data.FromFileAuto` / `leaves.LoadDataAuto`，`FormatAuto` 时读文件样本推断格式（见下表）。
   100|
   101|| 嗅探结果 | 典型内容 | 加载函数 |
   102||----------|----------|----------|
   103|| LIBSVM | `label 1:0.5 3:1.2` | `FromLIBSVM` |
   104|| 排序 TSV | `qid label feat...` | `LoadRankingTSV` |
   105|| TSV 末列 label | 无表头、末列为 label | `LoadDenseTSV` |
   106|| CSV | 有表头或启发式 label 列 | `FromCSV` |
   107|
   108|**变换**：`DefaultLoadOptions()` 已设 `AutoTransform: true`——`binary:logistic` 等返回概率，`reg:squarederror` 仍 raw。要 margin 请设 `AutoTransform: false` 或用 `predict.OutputMargin`。
   109|
   110|### XGBoost 3.x 模型加载（JSON / UBJ）
   111|
   112|`io.LoadFromFile` 自动识别格式（亦可用 `io.DetectFormat`）：
   113|
   114|| 格式 | 扩展名 | 说明 |
   115||------|--------|------|
   116|| XGBoost JSON | `.json` | 3.x `save_model` 默认；`base_score` 在 logistic 目标下自动转 margin |
   117|| XGBoost UBJSON | `.ubj` | 与 JSON 等价二进制；推理结果与 JSON 一致 |
   118|| XGBoost 二进制 | 无扩展 / `.bin` / `.model` | 经典 `xgb.Booster.save_model`；无魔数时按 header 探测 |
   119|| LightGBM | `.txt` / `.model` / `.json` | text 与 JSON 均支持 |
   120|| sklearn | `.pkl` / `.joblib` | pickle 魔数识别 |
   121|
   122|```go
   123|import "github.com/linkerlin/leaves/io"
   124|
   125|// JSON 或 UBJ：DefaultLoadOptions 已默认 AutoTransform
   126|m, err := io.LoadFromFile("model.ubj", io.DefaultLoadOptions())
   127|
   128|// 显式控制变换
   129|m, err = io.LoadFromFile("model.ubj", &io.LoadOptions{
   130|    AutoTransform:      false, // 输出 raw margin
   131|    LoadTransformation: true,  // 或强制对所有目标启用变换
   132|    Backend:            io.BackendAuto,

   236|#### 训练加速（T4+）
   237|
   238|hist / `gpu_hist` 路径上的加速栈（日志前缀 `[leaves/train] accel:`）：
   239|
   240|| 阶段 | 实现 | 默认 `auto`（≥3 万行 + WebGPU） | `LEAVES_TRAIN_ACCEL=webgpu` |
   241||------|------|-------------------------------|------------------------------|
   242|| 全局分箱 | 训练期每特征一次切点 + 行级 bin 缓存 | ✅ | ✅ |
   243|| 直方图累加 | CPU rowBin / WebGPU SelectAdd 批量 | **自动 gpu_hist** | GPU batch |
   244|| 增益扫描 | 纯 CPU / 批量 WebGPU gain | **批量 GPU**（GPU 路径） | 批量 GPU |
   245|| margin 预测 | 逐行 CPU / Born GPU `PredictDense` | `PredictMargins` ≥256 行 | ≥64 行 |
   246|
   247|`AccelMode=auto` 且 `TreeMethod=auto` 时：行数 **≥30000** 且 WebGPU 可用 → 自动解析为 `gpu_hist` + `webgpu`；小数据或显式 `TreeMethod=hist` 保持 CPU 路径。
   248|
   249|```go
   250|learner, _ := train.NewLearner(train.Config{
   251|    Objective:    train.ObjectiveRankNDCG,
   252|    TreeMethod:   train.TreeMethodGPUHist,
   253|    AccelMode:    train.AccelModeWebGPU, // 或依赖环境变量 LEAVES_TRAIN_ACCEL
   254|    NumThreads:   4,                     // 并行分裂评估 + GPU batch hist
   255|    HistBinPolicy: "global",              // 默认；per_node 恢复旧 per-node 切点
   256|    NumRound:     50,
   257|})
   258|_ = learner.Fit(dm)
   259|```
   260|
   261|环境变量：`LEAVES_TRAIN_ACCEL=auto|webgpu|born_cpu|cpu`（覆盖 `Config.AccelMode`）。
   262|
   263|**单调约束**（T5，`MonotoneConstraints []int`，对标 XGBoost `monotone_constraints`）：
   264|
   265|```go
   266|learner, _ := train.NewLearner(train.Config{
   267|    Objective:           "reg:squarederror",
   268|    MonotoneConstraints: []int{1, 0, -1}, // 特征 0 递增，2 递减
   269|    TreeMethod:          train.TreeMethodHist,
   270|})
   271|```
   272|
   273|**MSLTR 子集加速对比**（120 train queries / 5425 docs，50 轮 `rank:ndcg`，`i9` 类 Windows + WebGPU；复现：`go test ./train/... -run TestMSLTRTrainAccelBenchmark -v`）：
   274|
   275|| 模式 | 耗时 | train NDCG@10 | test NDCG@10 |
   276||------|------|---------------|--------------|
   277|| `cpu_hist` | **128s** | 0.706 | 0.397 |
   278|| `auto_hist` | ~142s | 0.697 | 0.397 |
   279|| `webgpu_hist` | 362s | 0.694 | 0.387 |
   280|
   281|实测环境：Windows + WebGPU，`rank:ndcg` 50 轮，5425 文档 / 136 特征，`NumThreads=4`（2026-06-13 本机复现）。
   282|
   283|- **`cpu_hist` 最快**：纯 CPU 增益扫描 + 全局 rowBin，无 Born 张量 / GPU 往返开销。
   284|- **`auto`**：与 `cpu_hist` 耗时接近（boosting 内不走 GPU margin）；GPU margin 仅在 `PredictMargins` / `EvalSet` 启用。
   285|- **`webgpu_hist`**：见下文「为何中小数据集上 GPU 未更快」。
   286|
   287|**为何 MSLTR 子集上 `webgpu_hist` 比 CPU 慢？**
   288|
   289|一部分是**数据规模偏小**，但不只是行数少：
   290|
   291|| 因素 | MSLTR 子集 | GPU 更划算的典型场景 |
   292||------|------------|----------------------|
   293|| 训练行数 | ~5k | 10 万+ |
   294|| 每节点样本 | 递归分裂后大量节点 < 64 | 根/浅层节点常有数千～上万样本 |
   295|| 特征数 | 136 | 数百列时 batch 收益更大 |
   296|
   297|更关键的是 **GPU 实际只覆盖了一小部分工作**。实测 `webgpu_hist` 一轮 Fit 日志示例：
   298|
   299|```
   300|hist_build(cpu=341320 webgpu=57024 total=398344)  → 仅 ~14% 直方图走 GPU
   301|gain_scan(webgpu=0 born_cpu=0 pure_cpu=324088)    → 增益扫描 0% GPU（NumThreads=4）
   302|```
   303|
   304|原因包括：
   305|
   306|1. **节点子集 < 64 样本** → 强制 CPU 直方图（浅层 `gpuHistMinSamples=64`，深层略降）；树越深，越多节点落在 CPU 路径。
   307|2. **中等节点混合策略**：样本数 < 4096 的节点仅前 32 特征走 GPU batch，其余 CPU rowBin（避免小节点 GPU 固定开销反超）。
   308|3. **`NumThreads>1`** → WebGPU 增益扫描仍禁用（小直方图上 Born/GPU 固定开销 > 纯 CPU 循环）；`webgpu_t1` 可走 GPU 增益扫描。
   309|4. **异构队列 + 批量 GPU gain scan**：hist batch 后同 mutex 内将 chunk（≤64 特征）的 f32 直方图 **一次 2D 上传**算增益，跳过逐特征 GPU 会话；`hasGain` 直出分裂点。
   310|5. **`NumThreads>1`**：GPU 路径增益走批量 scan（~53%）；CPU 路径特征仍纯 CPU 增益扫描。
   311|
   312|因此在该子集上出现 **362s vs 128s** 并不意外——不是 GPU 无效，而是**工作集太小 + GPU 命中率低 + 并行 hist 不走 GPU 增益扫描**。
   313|
   314|**何时 `webgpu_hist` 更可能反超 `cpu_hist`**
   315|
   316|需同时接近以下条件：
   317|
   318|- 训练行数 **10 万+**（或浅层节点样本经常 ≥ 几千）
   319|- 大 `EvalSet` / `PredictMargins` 走 GPU margin（≥64/256 行阈值）
   320|- 更多节点命中 GPU hist（可调大 `MaxBin`、或后续降低 `gpuHistMinSamples`）
   321|- 显式 `LEAVES_TRAIN_ACCEL=webgpu` + `TreeMethod=gpu_hist`；若需 GPU 增益扫描，暂用 `NumThreads=1`（与多线程确定性/trade-off 相关）
   322|
   323|**选型建议**：MSLTR 量级及同规模表格数据，优先 `cpu_hist` 或 `auto`；十万行以上再测 `webgpu_hist`。复现对比：
   324|
   325|```bash
   326|# MSLTR 子集（~5k 行）；默认 go test ./... 会跳过，需显式开启
   327|$env:LEAVES_BENCH=1
   328|go test ./train/... -run TestMSLTRTrainAccelBenchmark -v -timeout 45m
   329|
   330|# 大规模稠密回归交叉点（默认 5 万行 × 64 特征 × 10 轮）
   331|go test ./train/... -run TestLargeDenseTrainAccelBenchmark -v -timeout 60m
   332|# 可调规模：LEAVES_BENCH_ROWS / LEAVES_BENCH_COLS / LEAVES_BENCH_ROUNDS
   333|# 单模式：LEAVES_BENCH_ONLY=cpu_hist|webgpu_hist|webgpu_t1
   334|```
   335|
   336|大规模 benchmark 会输出 `hist_gpu=%`（直方图走 GPU 的比例）与完整 `accel summary`，便于判断交叉点。
   337|
   338|**大规模稠密回归对比**（`reg:squarederror`，64 特征，`MaxDepth=6`，Windows WebGPU；`TestLargeDenseTrainAccelBenchmark`）：
   339|
   340|| 行数 | 轮数 | `cpu_hist` | `webgpu_hist` | hist GPU 占比 | 结论 |
   341||------|------|------------|---------------|---------------|------|
   342|| 2 万 | 5 | **4.3s** | 8.0s | ~49% | CPU 更快（固定开销主导） |
   343|| 5 万 | 10 | **15.3s** | 28.1s | ~61% / gain ~53% | GPU 路径批量 gain；总耗时仍略慢于 CPU |
   344|| 5 万 | 10 | — | **29.0s** (`auto_smart`) | ~61% / gain ~53% | `AccelMode=auto` + `TreeMethod=auto` 自动解析为 `gpu_hist`+`webgpu` |
   345|| 10 万 | 10 | 63.2s | **59.2s** | ~49% | GPU 约快 6% |
   346|
   347|交叉点约在 **3–5 万行**（本机合成数据、浅层节点仍约一半走 CPU hist）。行数继续增大时收益受混合策略与 `NumThreads=4`（增益扫描走纯 CPU）限制；`LEAVES_BENCH_ONLY=webgpu_t1` 可启用 GPU 增益扫描，但单线程在大数据上通常更慢。
   348|
   349|### 计算底座
   350|
   351|推理与训练加速基于 [Born](https://github.com/born-ml/born)（CPU SIMD + WebGPU）。**训练**：见上文 [训练加速（T4+）](#训练加速t4)；`Fit` 结束日志含 `gain_scan` / `hist_build` / `accel margin` 分项。**推理**：`NativeEngine` 为 golden；`BackendAuto` 大 batch 可选用 `BackendBornGPU`（Windows）。
   352|
   353|**parity 门禁**：`TestBornParityFormatMatrix` 覆盖 LGB text/JSON、XGB bin/json/ubj、SK pickle × batch 1/16/256 × BornCPU/GPU，容差 `1e-5`（相对 Native）。
   354|
   355|```go
   356|m, _ := leaves.LoadFromFile("model.json", &io.LoadOptions{
   357|    Backend: io.BackendBornCPU,
   358|})
   359|```
   360|
   361|**Born 后端实测（`lg_breast_cancer`，i9-13900HX，Windows）** — `go test -bench=BenchmarkBreastCancerBackend -run=^$`：
   362|
   363|| batch | Native | BornCPU | BornGPU |
   364||-------|--------|---------|---------|
   365|| 1 | ~3 µs/op | ~1.9 ms/op | ~1.1 s/op（含 WebGPU 初始化） |
   366|| 16 | ~82 µs/op | ~4.3 ms/op | ~1.7 s/op |
   367|
   368|小森林张量路径有固定开销；**生产选型建议**：默认 `BackendAuto`（`DefaultLoadOptions()` 已启用 `AutoTransform`）；小 batch / 低延迟走 Native，大 batch 数值树再测 BornGPU。CI 门禁 `TestBenchGateBornCPUSlowerBatch1` 保证 batch=1 时 BornCPU ≥20× 慢于 Native。训练加速 benchmark：`LEAVES_BENCH=1 go test ./train/... -run TestMSLTRTrainAccelBenchmark -v`
   369|
   370|#### 后端选型速查
   371|
   372|| 场景 | 推荐 Backend | 说明 |
   373||------|--------------|------|
   374|| 在线单条 / batch≤8 | `BackendAuto` 或 `BackendNative` | 延迟优先 |
   375|| 批推理 ≥256 行、纯数值树 | `BackendAuto` + `Workload.BatchSize` | Windows 可试 BornGPU |
   376|| WASM / js | `BackendNative` | Born 在 js 回退 Native |
   377|| 含 LGB cat-small | `BackendNative` | Born 不支持 |
   378|
   379|```go
   380|m, _ := leaves.LoadFromFile("model.json", &io.LoadOptions{
   381|    Backend: io.BackendAuto,
   382|    Workload: tree.WorkloadHint{BatchSize: 1024, HasGPU: true},
   383|})
   384|```

   386|### Tree SHAP 与可解释性（v1.1+）
   387|
   388|推荐经 `io.LoadFromFile` 加载后使用 `model.Ensemble.Explain()`。`DefaultLoadOptions()` 对 logistic 模型返回**概率**；SHAP/contrib 仍在 margin 空间，与 `OutputMargin` / `predict.OutputContribution` 一致。
   389|
   390|```go
   391|import (
   392|	"github.com/linkerlin/leaves"
   393|	"github.com/linkerlin/leaves/explain"
   394|)
   395|
   396|m, _ := leaves.LoadFromFile("model.json", leaves.DefaultLoadOptions())
   397|
   398|x := [][]float64{{1.0, 2.0, 3.0}}
   399|contrib, _ := m.Explain().TreeSHAP(x)       // margin 空间 SHAP
   400|inter, _ := m.Explain().InteractionSHAP(x)  // 交互值矩阵
   401|mc, _ := m.Explain().TreeSHAPMulticlass(x)  // 多类：[sample][feature][class]
   402|base := m.Explain().ExpectedValue()         // 背景基线（全零特征）
   403|
   404|imp := m.Explain().Importance(explain.ImportanceGain, nil)
   405|text := m.Explain().DumpText(nil)
   406|dot := m.Explain().DumpDOT(nil)
   407|```
   408|
   409|说明：SHAP 采用 **Lundberg 快速 Tree SHAP**（`SumHess` 覆盖权重，tree_path_dependent）；背景基线仍为全零特征。与 XGBoost `pred_contribs` 在数值上可能略有差异，但可加性 `base + Σφ ≈ margin` 成立。
   410|
   411|**推荐（`predict.Request` 统一出口）**：末列 `bias` 为背景 margin（全零特征）；与 XGBoost `pred_contribs` **可加性一致**，逐元素分解可能不同。
   412|
   413|```go
   414|import "github.com/linkerlin/leaves/predict"
   415|
   416|nf := m.NFeatures()
   417|nRows := 1
   418|
   419|// 概率 / 变换后输出
   420|vals := make([]float64, nRows*m.NOutputGroups())
   421|_ = m.PredictWithRequest(predict.Request{
   422|    Matrix: predict.DenseMatrix{Values: flat, Rows: nRows, Cols: nf},
   423|    Output: predict.OutputValue,
   424|}, vals)
   425|
   426|// raw margin（未 sigmoid/softmax）
   427|margins := make([]float64, nRows*m.NRawOutputGroups())
   428|_ = m.PredictWithRequest(predict.Request{
   429|    Matrix: predict.DenseMatrix{Values: flat, Rows: nRows, Cols: nf},
   430|    Output: predict.OutputMargin,
   431|}, margins)
   432|
   433|// 叶节点索引（每棵树一个 int32）
   434|nTrees := m.NTrees()
   435|leaves := make([]int32, nRows*nTrees)
   436|_ = m.PredictWithRequest(predict.Request{
   437|    Matrix: predict.DenseMatrix{Values: flat, Rows: nRows, Cols: nf},
   438|    Output: predict.OutputLeaf,
   439|}, leaves)
   440|
   441|// Tree SHAP 贡献值（margin 空间；二分类 [sample][feature+bias]）
   442|out := make([]float64, (nf+1)*nRows)
   443|_ = m.PredictWithRequest(predict.Request{
   444|    Matrix: predict.DenseMatrix{Values: flat, Rows: nRows, Cols: nf},
   445|    Output: predict.OutputContribution, // 或 OutputApproxContribution / OutputInteraction
   446|}, out)
   447|
   448|// 多分类：扁平布局 [sample][class][feature+bias]，class 维 = NRawOutputGroups()
   449|ng := m.NRawOutputGroups()
   450|outMC := make([]float64, nRows*ng*(nf+1))
   451|```
   452|
   453|**bias 语义**：leaves 末列 = 全零特征的 **背景 margin**（`Explain().ExpectedValue()`）；与 XGBoost `pred_contribs` 可加性一致，逐元素分解可能不同。
   454|
   455|### 评估指标 `metrics/`
   456|
   457|内置 RMSE/MAE/AUC/LogLoss/MAPE/RMSLE/NDCG@k/MAP 等，名称与 XGBoost `eval_metric` 对齐（`metrics.Resolve`）：
   458|
   459|```go
   460|import "github.com/linkerlin/leaves/metrics"
   461|
   462|rmse, _ := metrics.RMSE{}.Evaluate(yTrue, yPred)
   463|m, _ := metrics.Resolve("ndcg@5", metrics.Options{Groups: []int{10, 10}})
   464|ndcg, _ := m.Evaluate(yTrue, yPred)
   465|```
   466|
   467|训练 `train.Config.EvalMetric` 支持同上名称；排序指标需 `data.GroupedMatrix` 提供 `Groups()`。
   468|
   469|### 排序学习（`rank:pairwise` / `rank:ndcg` / `rank:listwise`）
   470|
   471|对标 XGBoost LambdaMART：`rank:ndcg` / `rank:pairwise`。数据须为 `data.GroupedMatrix`（`Groups()` 为每个 query 的文档数）。
   472|
   473|| 目标 | 类型 | 说明 |
   474||------|------|------|
   475|| `rank:ndcg` | LambdaMART（XGBoost 兼容） | pairwise + \|ΔNDCG\| 缩放，优化排序指标 |
   476|| `rank:pairwise` | RankNet pairwise | 默认 **topk**（k=32）+ **lambdarank_normalization**（对标 XGBoost）；可选 `LambdaRankPairMethod=full\|mean` |
   477|| `rank:listwise` | **ListNet softmax CE**（leaves 原生） | 组内 q∝exp(label)、p=softmax(pred)，纯 listwise 交叉熵 |
   478|
   479|> `rank:ndcg` 与 `rank:listwise` 都面向 listwise 排序，但损失不同：前者对标 XGBoost LambdaMART，后者为 ListNet 式 softmax 目标（XGBoost 无同名目标）。
   480|
   481|```go
   482|import (
   483|    "github.com/linkerlin/leaves/data"
   484|    "github.com/linkerlin/leaves/train"
   485|)
   486|
   487|// TSV：qid label feat1 feat2 ...（同 qid 行连续）
   488|dm, _ := data.LoadRankingTSV("rank_train.tsv", "\t")
   489|
   490|// listwise（ListNet）
   491|learner, _ := train.NewLearner(train.Config{
   492|    Objective:    train.ObjectiveRankListwise,
   493|    NumRound:     40,
   494|    MaxDepth:     4,
   495|    LearningRate: 0.1,
   496|    NDCGK:        10,
   497|    EvalMetric:   "ndcg@10",
   498|})
   499|_ = learner.Fit(dm)
   500|
   501|// 或 RankNet pairwise（默认 topk 配对，k=32）
   502|learner, _ = train.NewLearner(train.Config{
   503|    Objective:    train.ObjectiveRankPairwise,
   504|    NumRound:     40,
   505|    MaxDepth:     4,
   506|    LearningRate: 0.1,
   507|    EvalMetric:   "ndcg@10",
   508|    TreeMethod:   train.TreeMethodHist,
   509|})
   510|_ = learner.Fit(dm)
   511|
   512|// 经典全配对（显式 full）
   513|learner, _ = train.NewLearner(train.Config{
   514|    Objective:            train.ObjectiveRankPairwise,
   515|    LambdaRankPairMethod: train.LambdaRankPairFull,
   516|    NumRound:             40,
   517|    MaxDepth:             4,
   518|    LearningRate:         0.1,
   519|    EvalMetric:           "ndcg@10",
   520|})
   521|
   522|// 可选：显式关闭 λ 归一化（默认 topk 下已开启）
   523|learner, _ = train.NewLearner(train.Config{
   524|    Objective:                  train.ObjectiveRankPairwise,
   525|    LambdaRankPairMethod:       train.LambdaRankPairTopK,
   526|    LambdaRankNumPairPerSample: 32,
   527|    // LambdaRankNormalization 默认 true；full 配对默认 false
   528|    NumRound:                   40,
   529|    MaxDepth:                   4,
   530|    LearningRate:               0.1,
   531|})
   532|_ = learner.Fit(dm)
   533|
   534|// 或 LambdaMART listwise（XGBoost 对标）
   535|learner, _ = train.NewLearner(train.Config{
   536|    Objective:    train.ObjectiveRankNDCG,
   537|    NumRound:     100,
   538|    MaxDepth:     6,
   539|    LearningRate: 0.1,
   540|    NDCGK:        10,
   541|    LambdaRankNorm: true,
   542|    TreeMethod:   train.TreeMethodHist,
   543|})
   544|_ = learner.Fit(dm)
   545|```

   581|### 训练回调与学习率调度（P3）
   582|
   583|```go
   584|learner, _ := train.NewLearner(train.Config{
   585|    Objective:    train.ObjectiveSquaredError,
   586|    NumRound:     20,
   587|    LearningRate: 0.3,
   588|    LRScheduler:  train.ExponentialLRScheduler(0.95), // 每轮 ×0.95
   589|    Callbacks: []train.TrainingCallback{
   590|        train.FuncCallback(func(ctx *train.CallbackContext) error {
   591|            // ctx.Round, ctx.LearningRate, ctx.TrainMetric, ctx.EvalMetric
   592|            return nil
   593|        }),
   594|    },
   595|})

   598|内置调度器：`ExponentialLRScheduler(gamma)`、`StepLRScheduler(every, factor)`。

   601|### 推理 profiling 与模型热更新（P3）
   602|
   603|```go

   605|if ne, ok := m.Engine().(*tree.NativeEngine); ok {
   606|    prof, err := tree.ProfileNativeDense(ne, vals, nrows, ncols, preds, 0)
   607|    _ = prof.Elapsed

   616|### int8 阈值量化与 parity 门禁
   617|
   618|数值分裂阈值按特征做 int8 仿射量化（127 级），**叶子值保持 float64**；分类节点不量化。  
   619|`quantize.Engine` 当前**不支持** `PredictLeafIndices*`（仅 margin 预测）。
   620|
   621|```go
   622|qf, _ := quantize.QuantizeForest(m.Forest(), quantize.Config{})
   623|res, err := quantize.CheckParityWithGate(m.Forest(), qf, samples, 0, quantize.DefaultGate())
   624|
   625|eng, _ := quantize.NewEngine(qf, nil, tree.TransformRaw, m.NOutputGroups())
   626|model.NewEnsemble(eng) // 可替换线上 Ensemble 引擎
   627|```

   687|## 局限
   688|
   689|  * LightGBM 模型：
   690|    * 变换函数支持有限（仅 sigmoid 与 softmax）
   691|  * XGBoost 模型：
   692|    * 变换函数支持有限（仅 sigmoid 与 softmax）

    39|## 〇、总纲
    40|
    41|### 费曼式一句话
    42|
    43|> leaves 是 **Go 生态里的 GBRT 推理与解释运行时**，并具备 **纯 Go 训练闭环**（T1–T5）：读 XGBoost/LightGBM 模型或自训练 → `ForestIR`/`ModelIR` → Native golden + Born 可选加速 → SHAP / 量化 / WASM 部署。
    44|
    45|### 产品定位
    46|
    47|| 维度 | 定位 |
    48||------|------|
    49|| **核心身份（v1.x）** | Go GBRT **推理与解释**运行时 |
    50|| **延伸身份（v2.x）** | 纯 Go **训练+推理一体化**（T1–T5 ✅） |
    51|| **不是** | XGBoost 逐行克隆、Born 式通用 DL 框架 |
    52|| **差异化** | 纯 Go 全链路 · XGBoost 3.x 互操作 · Tree SHAP · 训练→推理零切换 |
    53|| **Born 角色** | 张量计算底座（CPU SIMD + WebGPU）；GBDT 领域逻辑仍在 `tree/`/`train/` |
    54|
    55|### 核心命题
    56|
    57|**如何在不丢弃生态兼容性的前提下，以 `ModelIR` 为统一 IR、`NativeEngine` 为 golden，用 Born 张量栈替换 GoMLX，演进为支持 XGBoost 3.x、可解释性、Windows 原生 GPU 的 GBRT 运行时？**
    58|
    59|训练能力为 **v2.x 平行演进轨道**（见第十二章），不阻塞 v1.x 推理主线，但目标是在 Go 生态内实现**不依赖 Python/XGBoost 运行时**的独立完备训练闭环。
    60|
    61|### 设计原则
    62|
    63|1. **渐进增强，非推倒重来**：每个 Phase 产出可用的中间版本
    64|2. **接口稳定性**：根包 `Ensemble` 公开 API 保持兼容，内部实现可替换
    65|3. **Native 为正确性基准**：任何后端以 Native 为 golden；Born 是加速器，非真值来源
    66|4. **能力探测优于用户强选**：`BackendAuto` 按模型特征与 workload 自动选择引擎
    67|5. **IR 扩展，引擎跟随**：新特性先扩展 `ModelIR`/`ForestIR`，再更新引擎能力矩阵
    68|6. **参考 XGBoost，不做克隆**：学习架构思想，用 Go 惯用方式表达
    69|7. **训练与推理包分离**：`train/` 可依赖 `tree/`，`tree/` 不依赖 `train/`（Born 经 `tree/born_*.go` 直接读 `ForestIR`）
    70|8. **测试驱动演进**：每个 Phase 有 golden 测试与真实 testdata 回归
    71|9. **训练推理 IR 统一**：训练产出 `ModelIR`，`Fit → Save → Load → Predict` 必须 round-trip 可验证
    72|10. **训练插件化**：Objective / Booster / TreeUpdater 注册式扩展，对标 XGBoost Registry
    73|

# leaves

[![Version](https://img.shields.io/badge/version-v2.x--dev-blue.svg)](https://semver.org)
[![CI](https://github.com/linkerlin/leaves/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/linkerlin/leaves/actions/workflows/ci.yml)
[![Go Reference](https://pkg.go.dev/badge/github.com/linkerlin/leaves.svg)](https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/linkerlin/leaves)
[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT-green.svg)](LICENSE.md)

![Logo](logo.png)

## Introduction

`leaves` is a **pure Go** library for **inference and training of GBRT**
(Gradient Boosted Regression Trees) models. It loads XGBoost, LightGBM, and
scikit-learn models out of the box, and ships a complete in-Go training loop
(hist / exact, ranking, survival, tweedie, …) — no C bindings required.

**Recommended entry points**

- Inference: `leaves.LoadFromFile` (auto-transform on by default), or the
  legacy `leaves.LGEnsembleFromFile` / `leaves.XGEnsembleFromFile`.
- Training: `leaves.NewLearner` / `train.NewLearner`, `leaves.LoadDataAuto` /
  `data.FromFileAuto` (content sniffing), and the convenience helper
  `leaves.NewLearnerFromModelAndData` that infers the objective from a
  reference model.

See [docs/testdata-matrix.md](docs/testdata-matrix.md) for the regression
matrix, [演进计划.md](演进计划.md) for the roadmap (v4.3), and
[TODO.md](TODO.md) for the executable backlog.

## Features

### General

- Parallel batched prediction (dense / CSR).
- Sigmoid and softmax transformations; `DefaultLoadOptions()` enables
  **AutoTransform** by default.
- Decision-tree leaf-index extraction.
- **Content sniffing** for training data (LIBSVM, ranking TSV, CSV·TSV — the
  extension is only a fallback).

### [LightGBM](https://github.com/microsoft/LightGBM)

- Loads both `text` and `JSON` formats.
- `gbdt`, `rf` (random forest), and `dart` model families.
- Multiclass predictions.
- Optimised decision rules for categorical features (e.g. *one-hot* splits).
- Specialised fast paths for prediction-only scenarios.

### [XGBoost](https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost)

- Loads binary, JSON, and [UBJSON](https://github.com/jmckaskill/ubjson) models.
- `gbtree`, `gblinear`, and `dart` boosters; nested DART `gbtree.model` (3.x).
- Multiclass predictions, multi-output vector leaves, XGBoost categorical
  (`model.cats`) with round-trip export.
- Missing-value (`nan`) support.
- Auto-loaded `reg:gamma`, `count:poisson`, and `reg:tweedie` transformations
  (Exp + `base_score` log conversion).

### [scikit-learn](https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn) (experimental)

- Loads `pickle` (protocol `0`) trees.
- `sklearn.ensemble.GradientBoostingClassifier` supported.

### Training (v2.x, milestones T1–T5)

- `gbtree` / `dart` / `gblinear`; `hist` / `exact` / `gpu_hist` (`auto` selects
  by row count).
- Objectives: regression, binary / multiclass, gamma, poisson, tweedie,
  `survival:cox` / `survival:aft`, and ranking (`rank:ndcg` /
  `rank:pairwise` / `rank:listwise`).
- Cross-validation, early stopping, checkpointing, resume, monotonic
  constraints, `max_leaves` (lossguide), and `BatchedMatrix` for
  out-of-core DMatrix.
- Exporter: native `leaves.json` and **XGBoost 3.x JSON**.

### Deployment & observability

- int8 threshold quantization (with a parity gate).
- `Ensemble.Reload` for hot model rotation; per-batch inference profiling.
- [WASM demo](examples/wasm/README.md) (js / wasm, Native CPU),
  [HTTP embed demo](examples/http/README.md) (batched serving),
  and [train-from-model demo](examples/train_from_model/README.md).

## Installation

```sh
go install github.com/linkerlin/leaves@latest
```

Module path: `github.com/linkerlin/leaves` (Go 1.26+).
Tensors / GPU acceleration are powered by
[github.com/born-ml/born](https://github.com/born-ml/born).

## Quick start

The simplest path: load with `leaves.LoadFromFile` and call `PredictSingle`.

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"

	"github.com/linkerlin/leaves"
)

func main() {
	m, err := leaves.LoadFromFile("lightgbm_model.txt", leaves.DefaultLoadOptions())
	if err != nil {
		panic(err)
	}

	fvals := []float64{1.0, 2.0, 3.0}
	p, err := m.PredictSingle(fvals, 0)
	if err != nil {
		panic(err)
	}
	fmt.Printf("prediction for %v: %f\n", fvals, p)
}
```

Legacy aliases still work: `leaves.LGEnsembleFromFile(path, loadTransformation)`
and `leaves.XGEnsembleFromFile(...)`. Their behaviour is unchanged.

### Format auto-detection (v4.3)

`io.LoadFromFile` (and `io.DetectFormat`) picks the right backend from the
extension *and* a header probe. Classic XGBoost binaries have no magic
number, so they are recognised by content sniff; `.pkl` / `.joblib` resolve
to scikit-learn; numeric tables misnamed as `.txt` model files raise a
clear error.

| Format          | Extension                | Notes                                                                 |
| --------------- | ------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| XGBoost JSON    | `.json`                  | 3.x `save_model` default; `base_score` auto-shifted for logistic.     |
| XGBoost UBJSON  | `.ubj`                   | Binary equivalent of JSON; predictions bit-match the JSON path.       |
| XGBoost binary  | (none) / `.bin` / `.model` | Classic `xgb.Booster.save_model`; header probe when no magic number. |
| LightGBM        | `.txt` / `.model` / `.json` | Text and JSON both supported.                                       |
| scikit-learn    | `.pkl` / `.joblib`       | Pickle magic number recognised.                                       |

```go
import "github.com/linkerlin/leaves/io"

m, err := io.LoadFromFile("model.ubj", io.DefaultLoadOptions()) // AutoTransform = true

m, err = io.LoadFromFile("model.ubj", &io.LoadOptions{
	AutoTransform:      false, // raw margin output
	LoadTransformation: true,  // or force transforms for every objective
	Backend:            io.BackendAuto,
})
```

When `AutoTransform` and `LoadTransformation` are both `false`, `OutputValue`
and `OutputMargin` are both raw margin. `contrib` / SHAP are *always* in
margin space regardless of `AutoTransform`.

## Training API

```go
dm, _ := leaves.LoadDataAuto("train.tsv") // content sniff

learner, _ := leaves.NewLearner(leaves.TrainConfig{
	Objective: leaves.TrainObjectiveBinaryLogistic,
	NumRound:  50,
})
_ = learner.Fit(dm)
_ = learner.Save("out.leaves.json")
```

### Out-of-core DMatrix

```go
bm, _ := data.SplitDense(dense, batchRows) // or data.NewBatchedMatrix(...)
learner, _ := train.FitExternal(train.Config{
	Objective:     train.ObjectiveBinaryLogistic,
	TreeMethod:    train.TreeMethodHist,
	HistBinPolicy: "global",
	NumRound:      10,
}, bm)
```

### Checkpoint resume

```go
learner, _ := train.ResumeFit("ckpt.json", train.Config{
	Objective:  train.ObjectiveSquaredError,
	NumRound:   20, // continues past the checkpointed rounds
	TreeMethod: train.TreeMethodExact,
}, dm)
score, _ := learner.Eval(dm)
```

### Training data — auto-sniffing

`data.FromFileAuto` / `leaves.LoadDataAuto` reads a sample of the file and
selects a loader; the extension is only a fallback.

| Sniffed format     | Typical content                | Loader            |
| ------------------ | ------------------------------ | ----------------- |
| LIBSVM             | `label 1:0.5 3:1.2`            | `FromLIBSVM`      |
| Ranking TSV        | `qid label feat...`            | `LoadRankingTSV`  |
| TSV, label-last    | No header, label in last column | `LoadDenseTSV`  |
| CSV                | Header or heuristic label col  | `FromCSV`         |

### Infer the objective from a reference model

```go
learner, _ := leaves.NewLearnerFromModelAndData(
	"reference_model.json", // reads objective only (XGB JSON / UBJ / leaves.json)
	"train.tsv",            // LoadDataAuto content sniff
	leaves.TrainConfig{NumRound: 10, TreeMethod: leaves.TrainTreeMethodExact},
	data.DefaultFileLoadOptions(),
)
_ = learner.Save("out.leaves.json")
```

```bash
go run ./examples/train_from_model/ -model testdata/xgboost_smoke.json -data testdata/breast_cancer_train.tsv
```

### `survival:aft` interval censoring

```go
d, _ := data.NewDense(vals, n, nfeat, placeholderLabels, nil)
dm, _ := data.NewAFTDense(d, []data.AFTInterval{
	{1.5, 1.5},              // event
	{2, math.Inf(1)},        // right-censored
	{0, 4},                  // left-censored
	{1, 6},                  // interval-censored
})
learner, _ := train.NewLearner(train.Config{Objective: train.ObjectiveSurvivalAFT, ...})
_ = learner.Fit(dm)
```

### Monotonic constraints

```go
learner, _ := train.NewLearner(train.Config{
	Objective:           "reg:squarederror",
	MonotoneConstraints: []int{1, 0, -1}, // feature 0 increasing, 2 decreasing
	TreeMethod:          train.TreeMethodHist,
})
```

### Acceleration stack (T4+)

The hist / `gpu_hist` path is layered (log prefix `[leaves/train] accel:`):

| Stage            | Implementation                                          | Default `auto` (≥30k rows + WebGPU) | `LEAVES_TRAIN_ACCEL=webgpu` |
| ---------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- | --------------------------- |
| Global binning   | Per-feature cut points + row-level bin cache at fit time | ✅                                  | ✅                           |
| Histogram accum. | CPU `rowBin` / WebGPU `SelectAdd` batch                  | `gpu_hist`                          | GPU batch                    |
| Gain scan        | Pure CPU / batched WebGPU gain                           | Batched GPU (GPU path)              | Batched GPU                  |
| Margin predict   | Row-by-row CPU / Born GPU `PredictDense`                 | `PredictMargins` ≥ 256 rows         | ≥ 64 rows                    |

```go
learner, _ := train.NewLearner(train.Config{
	Objective:     train.ObjectiveRankNDCG,
	TreeMethod:    train.TreeMethodGPUHist,
	AccelMode:     train.AccelModeWebGPU, // or env LEAVES_TRAIN_ACCEL
	NumThreads:    4,
	HistBinPolicy: "global", // default; "per_node" restores per-node cuts
	NumRound:      50,
})
_ = learner.Fit(dm)
```

Environment override: `LEAVES_TRAIN_ACCEL=auto|webgpu|born_cpu|cpu`.

## Compute backend — [Born](https://github.com/born-ml/born)

Inference and training acceleration sit on
[Born](https://github.com/born-ml/born) (CPU SIMD + WebGPU). `NativeEngine`
is the golden reference; `BackendAuto` may dispatch to `BackendBornCPU` /
`BackendBornGPU` (Windows DX12) when it pays off.

```go
m, _ := leaves.LoadFromFile("model.json", &io.LoadOptions{
	Backend: io.BackendBornCPU,
})
```

The parity gate `TestBornParityFormatMatrix` covers LGB text/JSON, XGB
bin/json/ubj, scikit-learn pickle × batch `{1, 16, 256}` × `BornCPU` /
`BornGPU` at tolerance `1e-5` relative to Native.

### Backend selection cheat sheet

| Scenario                                       | Recommended `Backend`             | Why                              |
| ---------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------- | -------------------------------- |
| Online single / batch ≤ 8                      | `BackendAuto` or `BackendNative`  | Latency-first                    |
| Batch inference ≥ 256 rows, pure numeric trees | `BackendAuto` + `Workload` hint   | Windows: try `BackendBornGPU`    |
| WASM / js                                      | `BackendNative`                   | Born falls back to Native in js  |
| LightGBM `cat`-small features                  | `BackendNative`                   | Born does not support these yet  |

## Tree SHAP and explainability

`m.Explain()` exposes Lundberg fast Tree SHAP (with `SumHess` weight
coverage, `tree_path_dependent`); SHAP values are in margin space, matching
`OutputMargin` / `predict.OutputContribution`. `DefaultLoadOptions()` returns
*probabilities* for logistic objectives; contrib / SHAP remain in margin
space.

```go
import (
	"github.com/linkerlin/leaves"
	"github.com/linkerlin/leaves/explain"
)

m, _ := leaves.LoadFromFile("model.json", leaves.DefaultLoadOptions())

x := [][]float64{{1.0, 2.0, 3.0}}
contrib, _ := m.Explain().TreeSHAP(x)        // margin-space SHAP
inter, _ := m.Explain().InteractionSHAP(x)   // interaction matrix
mc, _ := m.Explain().TreeSHAPMulticlass(x)   // [sample][feature][class]
base := m.Explain().ExpectedValue()          // baseline (all-zero features)

imp := m.Explain().Importance(explain.ImportanceGain, nil)
text := m.Explain().DumpText(nil)
dot := m.Explain().DumpDOT(nil)
```

The unified output path is [`predict.Request`](predict/request.go):

```go
import "github.com/linkerlin/leaves/predict"

nf := m.NFeatures()
nRows := 1

// value (transformed output for logistic, raw for squared error)
vals := make([]float64, nRows*m.NOutputGroups())
_ = m.PredictWithRequest(predict.Request{
	Matrix: predict.DenseMatrix{Values: flat, Rows: nRows, Cols: nf},
	Output: predict.OutputValue,
}, vals)

// raw margin (before sigmoid / softmax)
margins := make([]float64, nRows*m.NRawOutputGroups())
_ = m.PredictWithRequest(predict.Request{
	Matrix: predict.DenseMatrix{Values: flat, Rows: nRows, Cols: nf},
	Output: predict.OutputMargin,
}, margins)

// leaf indices (int32 per tree)
nTrees := m.NTrees()
leaves := make([]int32, nRows*nTrees)
_ = m.PredictWithRequest(predict.Request{
	Matrix: predict.DenseMatrix{Values: flat, Rows: nRows, Cols: nf},
	Output: predict.OutputLeaf,
}, leaves)

// SHAP contributions (margin space; binary: [sample][feature+bias])
out := make([]float64, (nf+1)*nRows)
_ = m.PredictWithRequest(predict.Request{
	Matrix: predict.DenseMatrix{Values: flat, Rows: nRows, Cols: nf},
	Output: predict.OutputContribution, // or OutputApproxContribution / OutputInteraction
}, out)
```

`bias` semantics: the trailing column is the **background margin** at
all-zero features, matching the additivity convention of XGBoost's
`pred_contribs` (per-element decomposition may differ).

## Metrics — `metrics/`

Built-in RMSE / MAE / AUC / LogLoss / MAPE / RMSLE / NDCG@k / MAP, named to
match XGBoost's `eval_metric`:

```go
import "github.com/linkerlin/leaves/metrics"

rmse, _ := metrics.RMSE{}.Evaluate(yTrue, yPred)
m, _ := metrics.Resolve("ndcg@5", metrics.Options{Groups: []int{10, 10}})
ndcg, _ := m.Evaluate(yTrue, yPred)
```

Ranking metrics need a `data.GroupedMatrix` exposing `Groups()`.

## Learning to rank

XGBoost-compatible LambdaMART plus a native listwise head:

| Objective        | Family                                       | Notes                                                                       |
| ---------------- | -------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `rank:ndcg`      | LambdaMART (XGBoost-compatible)              | Pairwise with \|ΔNDCG\| weighting; targets the ranking metric directly.      |
| `rank:pairwise`  | RankNet pairwise                             | Defaults to **top-k** (k = 32) + `lambdarank_normalization` (XGB-aligned).  |
| `rank:listwise`  | **ListNet softmax CE** (leaves native)       | `q ∝ exp(label)`, `p = softmax(pred)`, pure listwise cross-entropy.         |

```go
import (
	"github.com/linkerlin/leaves/data"
	"github.com/linkerlin/leaves/train"
)

dm, _ := data.LoadRankingTSV("rank_train.tsv", "\t") // qid label feat1 feat2 ...

learner, _ := train.NewLearner(train.Config{
	Objective:    train.ObjectiveRankNDCG, // or ObjectiveRankPairwise / ObjectiveRankListwise
	NumRound:     40,
	MaxDepth:     4,
	LearningRate: 0.1,
	NDCGK:        10,
	EvalMetric:   "ndcg@10",
})
_ = learner.Fit(dm)
```

End-to-end MovieLens 100K demo (train → save → top-K recommend):
[`demos/movielens/README.md`](demos/movielens/README.md).

```bash
cd testdata && python gen_rank_movielens.py
go run ./demos/movielens/cmd/train
go run ./demos/movielens/cmd/recommend -group 0 -topk 10
go test ./train/... -run TestRankMovieLens -v
```

## Callbacks and LR schedulers

```go
learner, _ := train.NewLearner(train.Config{
	Objective:    train.ObjectiveSquaredError,
	NumRound:     20,
	LearningRate: 0.3,
	LRScheduler:  train.ExponentialLRScheduler(0.95), // ×0.95 per round
	Callbacks: []train.TrainingCallback{
		train.FuncCallback(func(ctx *train.CallbackContext) error {
			// ctx.Round, ctx.LearningRate, ctx.TrainMetric, ctx.EvalMetric
			return nil
		}),
	},
})
```

Built-in schedulers: `ExponentialLRScheduler(gamma)` and
`StepLRScheduler(every, factor)`. `CallbackContext` also fills
`EvalMetric` / `EvalMetricOK` when `EvalSet` + `EvalMetric` are configured
(no `EarlyStop` required).

## Inference profiling and hot reload

```go
if ne, ok := m.Engine().(*tree.NativeEngine); ok {
	prof, err := tree.ProfileNativeDense(ne, vals, nrows, ncols, preds, 0)
	_ = prof.Elapsed
}

// Atomic model rotation in production
_ = m.Reload("/path/to/new.model", io.DefaultLoadOptions())
```

`tree.ProfileWalkStats` reports per-sample tree-walk depth without running
the full prediction.

## int8 threshold quantization

Numerical split thresholds are int8-affine quantised per feature
(127 levels); **leaf values stay float64** and categorical nodes are not
quantised. `quantize.Engine` does not support `PredictLeafIndices*`
(margin only).

```go
qf, _ := quantize.QuantizeForest(m.Forest(), quantize.Config{})
res, err := quantize.CheckParityWithGate(m.Forest(), qf, samples, 0, quantize.DefaultGate())

eng, _ := quantize.NewEngine(qf, nil, tree.TransformRaw, m.NOutputGroups())
model.NewEnsemble(eng) // swap the live engine
```

## Documentation

| Document                                      | Description                                                  |
| --------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ |
| [godoc](https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/linkerlin/leaves) | API reference                                         |
| [演进计划.md](演进计划.md)                       | End-to-end roadmap (**v4.3**, in sync with code)             |
| [TODO.md](TODO.md)                            | Executable backlog (P0–T5 + v3.1 cleared)                    |
| [NOTES.md](NOTES.md)                          | Version and compatibility notes (incl. v4.3 `AutoTransform`) |
| [compatibility.md](compatibility.md)          | External GBRT library correctness sweep                      |
| [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md)                        | Project conventions & compute substrate decisions            |
| [docs/testdata-matrix.md](docs/testdata-matrix.md) | Regression test matrix                                    |
| [docs/benchmark-baseline.md](docs/benchmark-baseline.md) | Benchmarks and CI gates                                |
| [examples/wasm/README.md](examples/wasm/README.md)   | WASM deployment guide                                   |
| [examples/http/README.md](examples/http/README.md)   | HTTP embed batch-prediction demo                       |
| [examples/train_from_model/README.md](examples/train_from_model/README.md) | Sniff + objective-inferred training demo |
| [demos/movielens/README.md](demos/movielens/README.md) | MovieLens 100K ranking walkthrough                  |
| [leaves_test.go](leaves_test.go)              | More usage examples                                          |

## Compatibility

Most features are regression-tested against multiple GBRT library
versions. The full correctness matrix (XGBoost 0.72–0.90, LightGBM
2.0.10–2.3.0) lives in [compatibility.md](compatibility.md). New behaviour
and back-compat notes are kept in [NOTES.md](NOTES.md).

## Performance

Single-thread, batch 1000, on a 2017 15" MacBook Pro (2.9 GHz Core i7,
16 GB 2133 MHz LPDDR3). C-API numbers come from Python bindings; per-call
overhead is negligible in the batched regime. `go test -bench` drives the
go side; see [benchmark/](benchmark/) and
[testdata/README.md](testdata/README.md) for data prep.

Single-thread:

| Case                                                              | Features | Trees | Batch | C API | _leaves_ |
| ----------------------------------------------------------------- | -------- | ----- | ----- | ----- | -------- |
| LightGBM [MS LTR](https://github.com/microsoft/LightGBM/blob/master/docs/Experiments.rst#comparison-experiment) | 137      | 500   | 1000  | 49 ms | 51 ms    |
| LightGBM [Higgs](https://github.com/microsoft/LightGBM/blob/master/docs/Experiments.rst#comparison-experiment)   | 28       | 500   | 1000  | 50 ms | 50 ms    |
| LightGBM KDD Cup 99*                                              | 41       | 1200  | 1000  | 70 ms | 85 ms    |
| XGBoost Higgs                                                     | 28       | 500   | 1000  | 44 ms | 50 ms    |

Four threads:

| Case                                                              | Features | Trees | Batch | C API | _leaves_ |
| ----------------------------------------------------------------- | -------- | ----- | ----- | ----- | -------- |
| LightGBM [MS LTR](https://github.com/microsoft/LightGBM/blob/master/docs/Experiments.rst#comparison-experiment) | 137      | 500   | 1000  | 14 ms | 14 ms    |
| LightGBM [Higgs](https://github.com/microsoft/LightGBM/blob/master/docs/Experiments.rst#comparison-experiment)   | 28       | 500   | 1000  | 14 ms | 14 ms    |
| LightGBM KDD Cup 99*                                              | 41       | 1200  | 1000  | 19 ms | 24 ms    |
| XGBoost Higgs                                                     | 28       | 500   | 1000  | ?     | 14 ms    |

(`?`) XGBoost multithreaded prediction is not reachable through the
Python bindings. (`*`) KDD Cup 99 mixes continuous and categorical
features.

For a Born-backend microbench (Windows / WebGPU) see
[docs/benchmark-baseline.md](docs/benchmark-baseline.md).

## Limitations

- **LightGBM** — limited set of transformations (sigmoid and softmax only).
- **XGBoost** — limited set of transformations (sigmoid and softmax only);
  floating-point rounding means C-API and `_leaves_` predictions may differ
  by a handful of ULPs.
- **scikit-learn** — no transformation support (output is the raw score from
  `GradientBoostingClassifier.decision_function`); only pickle protocol `0`;
  same float-rounding caveat as XGBoost.

## Roadmap

The end-to-end roadmap (parity with XGBoost 3.3 across training,
inference, serving, and observability) lives in
[演进计划.md](演进计划.md) — **v4.3** lands format sniffing,
`AutoTransform` by default, and a training convenience API. The
executable backlog is in [TODO.md](TODO.md) (P0–T5 are closed).

For questions or collaboration, email **steper@foxmail.com**.

# leaves

[![Version](https://img.shields.io/badge/version-v2.x--dev-blue.svg)](https://semver.org)
[![CI](https://github.com/linkerlin/leaves/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/linkerlin/leaves/actions/workflows/ci.yml)
[![Go Reference](https://pkg.go.dev/badge/github.com/linkerlin/leaves.svg)](https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/linkerlin/leaves)
[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT-green.svg)](LICENSE.md)

![Logo](logo.png)

## Introduction

`leaves` is a **pure Go** library for **inference and training of GBRT**
(Gradient Boosted Regression Trees) models. It loads XGBoost, LightGBM, and
scikit-learn models out of the box, and ships a complete in-Go training loop
(hist / exact, ranking, survival, tweedie, …) — no C bindings required.

**Recommended entry points**

- Inference: `leaves.LoadFromFile` (auto-transform on by default), or the
  legacy `leaves.LGEnsembleFromFile` / `leaves.XGEnsembleFromFile`.
- Training: `leaves.NewLearner` / `train.NewLearner`, `leaves.LoadDataAuto` /
  `data.FromFileAuto` (content sniffing), and the convenience helper
  `leaves.NewLearnerFromModelAndData` that infers the objective from a
  reference model.

See [docs/testdata-matrix.md](docs/testdata-matrix.md) for the regression
matrix, [演进计划.md](演进计划.md) for the roadmap (v4.3), and
[TODO.md](TODO.md) for the executable backlog.

## Features

### General

- Parallel batched prediction (dense / CSR).
- Sigmoid and softmax transformations; `DefaultLoadOptions()` enables
  **AutoTransform** by default.
- Decision-tree leaf-index extraction.
- **Content sniffing** for training data (LIBSVM, ranking TSV, CSV·TSV — the
  extension is only a fallback).

### [LightGBM](https://github.com/microsoft/LightGBM)

- Loads both `text` and `JSON` formats.
- `gbdt`, `rf` (random forest), and `dart` model families.
- Multiclass predictions.
- Optimised decision rules for categorical features (e.g. *one-hot* splits).
- Specialised fast paths for prediction-only scenarios.

### [XGBoost](https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost)

- Loads binary, JSON, and [UBJSON](https://github.com/jmckaskill/ubjson) models.
- `gbtree`, `gblinear`, and `dart` boosters; nested DART `gbtree.model` (3.x).
- Multiclass predictions, multi-output vector leaves, XGBoost categorical
  (`model.cats`) with round-trip export.
- Missing-value (`nan`) support.
- Auto-loaded `reg:gamma`, `count:poisson`, and `reg:tweedie` transformations
  (Exp + `base_score` log conversion).

### [scikit-learn](https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn) (experimental)

- Loads `pickle` (protocol `0`) trees.
- `sklearn.ensemble.GradientBoostingClassifier` supported.

### Training (v2.x, milestones T1–T5)

- `gbtree` / `dart` / `gblinear`; `hist` / `exact` / `gpu_hist` (`auto` selects
  by row count).
- Objectives: regression, binary / multiclass, gamma, poisson, tweedie,
  `survival:cox` / `survival:aft`, and ranking (`rank:ndcg` /
  `rank:pairwise` / `rank:listwise`).
- Cross-validation, early stopping, checkpointing, resume, monotonic
  constraints, `max_leaves` (lossguide), and `BatchedMatrix` for
  out-of-core DMatrix.
- Exporter: native `leaves.json` and **XGBoost 3.x JSON**.

### Deployment & observability

- int8 threshold quantization (with a parity gate).
- `Ensemble.Reload` for hot model rotation; per-batch inference profiling.
- [WASM demo](examples/wasm/README.md) (js / wasm, Native CPU),
  [HTTP embed demo](examples/http/README.md) (batched serving),
  and [train-from-model demo](examples/train_from_model/README.md).

## Installation

```sh
go install github.com/linkerlin/leaves@latest
```

Module path: `github.com/linkerlin/leaves` (Go 1.26+).
Tensors / GPU acceleration are powered by
[github.com/born-ml/born](https://github.com/born-ml/born).

## Quick start

The simplest path: load with `leaves.LoadFromFile` and call `PredictSingle`.

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"

	"github.com/linkerlin/leaves"
)

func main() {
	m, err := leaves.LoadFromFile("lightgbm_model.txt", leaves.DefaultLoadOptions())
	if err != nil {
		panic(err)
	}

	fvals := []float64{1.0, 2.0, 3.0}
	p, err := m.PredictSingle(fvals, 0)
	if err != nil {
		panic(err)
	}
	fmt.Printf("prediction for %v: %f\n", fvals, p)
}
```

Legacy aliases still work: `leaves.LGEnsembleFromFile(path, loadTransformation)`
and `leaves.XGEnsembleFromFile(...)`. Their behaviour is unchanged.

### Format auto-detection (v4.3)

`io.LoadFromFile` (and `io.DetectFormat`) picks the right backend from the
extension *and* a header probe. Classic XGBoost binaries have no magic
number, so they are recognised by content sniff; `.pkl` / `.joblib` resolve
to scikit-learn; numeric tables misnamed as `.txt` model files raise a
clear error.

| Format          | Extension                | Notes                                                                 |
| --------------- | ------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| XGBoost JSON    | `.json`                  | 3.x `save_model` default; `base_score` auto-shifted for logistic.     |
| XGBoost UBJSON  | `.ubj`                   | Binary equivalent of JSON; predictions bit-match the JSON path.       |
| XGBoost binary  | (none) / `.bin` / `.model` | Classic `xgb.Booster.save_model`; header probe when no magic number. |
| LightGBM        | `.txt` / `.model` / `.json` | Text and JSON both supported.                                       |
| scikit-learn    | `.pkl` / `.joblib`       | Pickle magic number recognised.                                       |

```go
import "github.com/linkerlin/leaves/io"

m, err := io.LoadFromFile("model.ubj", io.DefaultLoadOptions()) // AutoTransform = true

m, err = io.LoadFromFile("model.ubj", &io.LoadOptions{
	AutoTransform:      false, // raw margin output
	LoadTransformation: true,  // or force transforms for every objective
	Backend:            io.BackendAuto,
})
```

When `AutoTransform` and `LoadTransformation` are both `false`, `OutputValue`
and `OutputMargin` are both raw margin. `contrib` / SHAP are *always* in
margin space regardless of `AutoTransform`.

## Training API

```go
dm, _ := leaves.LoadDataAuto("train.tsv") // content sniff

learner, _ := leaves.NewLearner(leaves.TrainConfig{
	Objective: leaves.TrainObjectiveBinaryLogistic,
	NumRound:  50,
})
_ = learner.Fit(dm)
_ = learner.Save("out.leaves.json")
```

### Out-of-core DMatrix

```go
bm, _ := data.SplitDense(dense, batchRows) // or data.NewBatchedMatrix(...)
learner, _ := train.FitExternal(train.Config{
	Objective:     train.ObjectiveBinaryLogistic,
	TreeMethod:    train.TreeMethodHist,
	HistBinPolicy: "global",
	NumRound:      10,
}, bm)
```

### Checkpoint resume

```go
learner, _ := train.ResumeFit("ckpt.json", train.Config{
	Objective:  train.ObjectiveSquaredError,
	NumRound:   20, // continues past the checkpointed rounds
	TreeMethod: train.TreeMethodExact,
}, dm)
score, _ := learner.Eval(dm)
```

### Training data — auto-sniffing

`data.FromFileAuto` / `leaves.LoadDataAuto` reads a sample of the file and
selects a loader; the extension is only a fallback.

| Sniffed format     | Typical content                | Loader            |
| ------------------ | ------------------------------ | ----------------- |
| LIBSVM             | `label 1:0.5 3:1.2`            | `FromLIBSVM`      |
| Ranking TSV        | `qid label feat...`            | `LoadRankingTSV`  |
| TSV, label-last    | No header, label in last column | `LoadDenseTSV`  |
| CSV                | Header or heuristic label col  | `FromCSV`         |

### Infer the objective from a reference model

```go
learner, _ := leaves.NewLearnerFromModelAndData(
	"reference_model.json", // reads objective only (XGB JSON / UBJ / leaves.json)
	"train.tsv",            // LoadDataAuto content sniff
	leaves.TrainConfig{NumRound: 10, TreeMethod: leaves.TrainTreeMethodExact},
	data.DefaultFileLoadOptions(),
)
_ = learner.Save("out.leaves.json")
```

```bash
go run ./examples/train_from_model/ -model testdata/xgboost_smoke.json -data testdata/breast_cancer_train.tsv
```

### `survival:aft` interval censoring

```go
d, _ := data.NewDense(vals, n, nfeat, placeholderLabels, nil)
dm, _ := data.NewAFTDense(d, []data.AFTInterval{
	{1.5, 1.5},              // event
	{2, math.Inf(1)},        // right-censored
	{0, 4},                  // left-censored
	{1, 6},                  // interval-censored
})
learner, _ := train.NewLearner(train.Config{Objective: train.ObjectiveSurvivalAFT, ...})
_ = learner.Fit(dm)
```

### Monotonic constraints

```go
learner, _ := train.NewLearner(train.Config{
	Objective:           "reg:squarederror",
	MonotoneConstraints: []int{1, 0, -1}, // feature 0 increasing, 2 decreasing
	TreeMethod:          train.TreeMethodHist,
})
```

### Acceleration stack (T4+)

The hist / `gpu_hist` path is layered (log prefix `[leaves/train] accel:`):

| Stage            | Implementation                                          | Default `auto` (≥30k rows + WebGPU) | `LEAVES_TRAIN_ACCEL=webgpu` |
| ---------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- | --------------------------- |
| Global binning   | Per-feature cut points + row-level bin cache at fit time | ✅                                  | ✅                           |
| Histogram accum. | CPU `rowBin` / WebGPU `SelectAdd` batch                  | `gpu_hist`                          | GPU batch                    |
| Gain scan        | Pure CPU / batched WebGPU gain                           | Batched GPU (GPU path)              | Batched GPU                  |
| Margin predict   | Row-by-row CPU / Born GPU `PredictDense`                 | `PredictMargins` ≥ 256 rows         | ≥ 64 rows                    |

```go
learner, _ := train.NewLearner(train.Config{
	Objective:     train.ObjectiveRankNDCG,
	TreeMethod:    train.TreeMethodGPUHist,
	AccelMode:     train.AccelModeWebGPU, // or env LEAVES_TRAIN_ACCEL
	NumThreads:    4,
	HistBinPolicy: "global", // default; "per_node" restores per-node cuts
	NumRound:      50,
})
_ = learner.Fit(dm)
```

Environment override: `LEAVES_TRAIN_ACCEL=auto|webgpu|born_cpu|cpu`.

## Compute backend — [Born](https://github.com/born-ml/born)

Inference and training acceleration sit on
[Born](https://github.com/born-ml/born) (CPU SIMD + WebGPU). `NativeEngine`
is the golden reference; `BackendAuto` may dispatch to `BackendBornCPU` /
`BackendBornGPU` (Windows DX12) when it pays off.

```go
m, _ := leaves.LoadFromFile("model.json", &io.LoadOptions{
	Backend: io.BackendBornCPU,
})
```

The parity gate `TestBornParityFormatMatrix` covers LGB text/JSON, XGB
bin/json/ubj, scikit-learn pickle × batch `{1, 16, 256}` × `BornCPU` /
`BornGPU` at tolerance `1e-5` relative to Native.

### Backend selection cheat sheet

| Scenario                                       | Recommended `Backend`             | Why                              |
| ---------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------- | -------------------------------- |
| Online single / batch ≤ 8                      | `BackendAuto` or `BackendNative`  | Latency-first                    |
| Batch inference ≥ 256 rows, pure numeric trees | `BackendAuto` + `Workload` hint   | Windows: try `BackendBornGPU`    |
| WASM / js                                      | `BackendNative`                   | Born falls back to Native in js  |
| LightGBM `cat`-small features                  | `BackendNative`                   | Born does not support these yet  |

## Tree SHAP and explainability

`m.Explain()` exposes Lundberg fast Tree SHAP (with `SumHess` weight
coverage, `tree_path_dependent`); SHAP values are in margin space, matching
`OutputMargin` / `predict.OutputContribution`. `DefaultLoadOptions()` returns
*probabilities* for logistic objectives; contrib / SHAP remain in margin
space.

```go
import (
	"github.com/linkerlin/leaves"
	"github.com/linkerlin/leaves/explain"
)

m, _ := leaves.LoadFromFile("model.json", leaves.DefaultLoadOptions())

x := [][]float64{{1.0, 2.0, 3.0}}
contrib, _ := m.Explain().TreeSHAP(x)        // margin-space SHAP
inter, _ := m.Explain().InteractionSHAP(x)   // interaction matrix
mc, _ := m.Explain().TreeSHAPMulticlass(x)   // [sample][feature][class]
base := m.Explain().ExpectedValue()          // baseline (all-zero features)

imp := m.Explain().Importance(explain.ImportanceGain, nil)
text := m.Explain().DumpText(nil)
dot := m.Explain().DumpDOT(nil)
```

The unified output path is [`predict.Request`](predict/request.go):

```go
import "github.com/linkerlin/leaves/predict"

nf := m.NFeatures()
nRows := 1

// value (transformed output for logistic, raw for squared error)
vals := make([]float64, nRows*m.NOutputGroups())
_ = m.PredictWithRequest(predict.Request{
	Matrix: predict.DenseMatrix{Values: flat, Rows: nRows, Cols: nf},
	Output: predict.OutputValue,
}, vals)

// raw margin (before sigmoid / softmax)
margins := make([]float64, nRows*m.NRawOutputGroups())
_ = m.PredictWithRequest(predict.Request{
	Matrix: predict.DenseMatrix{Values: flat, Rows: nRows, Cols: nf},
	Output: predict.OutputMargin,
}, margins)

// leaf indices (int32 per tree)
nTrees := m.NTrees()
leaves := make([]int32, nRows*nTrees)
_ = m.PredictWithRequest(predict.Request{
	Matrix: predict.DenseMatrix{Values: flat, Rows: nRows, Cols: nf},
	Output: predict.OutputLeaf,
}, leaves)

// SHAP contributions (margin space; binary: [sample][feature+bias])
out := make([]float64, (nf+1)*nRows)
_ = m.PredictWithRequest(predict.Request{
	Matrix: predict.DenseMatrix{Values: flat, Rows: nRows, Cols: nf},
	Output: predict.OutputContribution, // or OutputApproxContribution / OutputInteraction
}, out)
```

`bias` semantics: the trailing column is the **background margin** at
all-zero features, matching the additivity convention of XGBoost's
`pred_contribs` (per-element decomposition may differ).

## Metrics — `metrics/`

Built-in RMSE / MAE / AUC / LogLoss / MAPE / RMSLE / NDCG@k / MAP, named to
match XGBoost's `eval_metric`:

```go
import "github.com/linkerlin/leaves/metrics"

rmse, _ := metrics.RMSE{}.Evaluate(yTrue, yPred)
m, _ := metrics.Resolve("ndcg@5", metrics.Options{Groups: []int{10, 10}})
ndcg, _ := m.Evaluate(yTrue, yPred)
```

Ranking metrics need a `data.GroupedMatrix` exposing `Groups()`.

## Learning to rank

XGBoost-compatible LambdaMART plus a native listwise head:

| Objective        | Family                                       | Notes                                                                       |
| ---------------- | -------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `rank:ndcg`      | LambdaMART (XGBoost-compatible)              | Pairwise with \|ΔNDCG\| weighting; targets the ranking metric directly.      |
| `rank:pairwise`  | RankNet pairwise                             | Defaults to **top-k** (k = 32) + `lambdarank_normalization` (XGB-aligned).  |
| `rank:listwise`  | **ListNet softmax CE** (leaves native)       | `q ∝ exp(label)`, `p = softmax(pred)`, pure listwise cross-entropy.         |

```go
import (
	"github.com/linkerlin/leaves/data"
	"github.com/linkerlin/leaves/train"
)

dm, _ := data.LoadRankingTSV("rank_train.tsv", "\t") // qid label feat1 feat2 ...

learner, _ := train.NewLearner(train.Config{
	Objective:    train.ObjectiveRankNDCG, // or ObjectiveRankPairwise / ObjectiveRankListwise
	NumRound:     40,
	MaxDepth:     4,
	LearningRate: 0.1,
	NDCGK:        10,
	EvalMetric:   "ndcg@10",
})
_ = learner.Fit(dm)
```

End-to-end MovieLens 100K demo (train → save → top-K recommend):
[`demos/movielens/README.md`](demos/movielens/README.md).

```bash
cd testdata && python gen_rank_movielens.py
go run ./demos/movielens/cmd/train
go run ./demos/movielens/cmd/recommend -group 0 -topk 10
go test ./train/... -run TestRankMovieLens -v
```

## Callbacks and LR schedulers

```go
learner, _ := train.NewLearner(train.Config{
	Objective:    train.ObjectiveSquaredError,
	NumRound:     20,
	LearningRate: 0.3,
	LRScheduler:  train.ExponentialLRScheduler(0.95), // ×0.95 per round
	Callbacks: []train.TrainingCallback{
		train.FuncCallback(func(ctx *train.CallbackContext) error {
			// ctx.Round, ctx.LearningRate, ctx.TrainMetric, ctx.EvalMetric
			return nil
		}),
	},
})
```

Built-in schedulers: `ExponentialLRScheduler(gamma)` and
`StepLRScheduler(every, factor)`. `CallbackContext` also fills
`EvalMetric` / `EvalMetricOK` when `EvalSet` + `EvalMetric` are configured
(no `EarlyStop` required).

## Inference profiling and hot reload

```go
if ne, ok := m.Engine().(*tree.NativeEngine); ok {
	prof, err := tree.ProfileNativeDense(ne, vals, nrows, ncols, preds, 0)
	_ = prof.Elapsed
}

// Atomic model rotation in production
_ = m.Reload("/path/to/new.model", io.DefaultLoadOptions())
```

`tree.ProfileWalkStats` reports per-sample tree-walk depth without running
the full prediction.

## int8 threshold quantization

Numerical split thresholds are int8-affine quantised per feature
(127 levels); **leaf values stay float64** and categorical nodes are not
quantised. `quantize.Engine` does not support `PredictLeafIndices*`
(margin only).

```go
qf, _ := quantize.QuantizeForest(m.Forest(), quantize.Config{})
res, err := quantize.CheckParityWithGate(m.Forest(), qf, samples, 0, quantize.DefaultGate())

eng, _ := quantize.NewEngine(qf, nil, tree.TransformRaw, m.NOutputGroups())
model.NewEnsemble(eng) // swap the live engine
```

## Compatibility

Most features are regression-tested against multiple GBRT library
versions. The full correctness matrix (XGBoost 0.72–0.90, LightGBM
2.0.10–2.3.0) lives in [compatibility.md](compatibility.md). New behaviour
and back-compat notes are kept in [NOTES.md](NOTES.md).

## Performance

Single-thread, batch 1000, on a 2017 15" MacBook Pro (2.9 GHz Core i7,
16 GB 2133 MHz LPDDR3). C-API numbers come from Python bindings; per-call
overhead is negligible in the batched regime. `go test -bench` drives the
go side; see [benchmark/](benchmark/) and
[testdata/README.md](testdata/README.md) for data prep.

Single-thread:

| Case                                                              | Features | Trees | Batch | C API | _leaves_ |
| ----------------------------------------------------------------- | -------- | ----- | ----- | ----- | -------- |
| LightGBM [MS LTR](https://github.com/microsoft/LightGBM/blob/master/docs/Experiments.rst#comparison-experiment) | 137      | 500   | 1000  | 49 ms | 51 ms    |
| LightGBM [Higgs](https://github.com/microsoft/LightGBM/blob/master/docs/Experiments.rst#comparison-experiment)   | 28       | 500   | 1000  | 50 ms | 50 ms    |
| LightGBM KDD Cup 99*                                              | 41       | 1200  | 1000  | 70 ms | 85 ms    |
| XGBoost Higgs                                                     | 28       | 500   | 1000  | 44 ms | 50 ms    |

Four threads:

| Case                                                              | Features | Trees | Batch | C API | _leaves_ |
| ----------------------------------------------------------------- | -------- | ----- | ----- | ----- | -------- |
| LightGBM [MS LTR](https://github.com/microsoft/LightGBM/blob/master/docs/Experiments.rst#comparison-experiment) | 137      | 500   | 1000  | 14 ms | 14 ms    |
| LightGBM [Higgs](https://github.com/microsoft/LightGBM/blob/master/docs/Experiments.rst#comparison-experiment)   | 28       | 500   | 1000  | 14 ms | 14 ms    |
| LightGBM KDD Cup 99*                                              | 41       | 1200  | 1000  | 19 ms | 24 ms    |
| XGBoost Higgs                                                     | 28       | 500   | 1000  | ?     | 14 ms    |

(`?`) XGBoost multithreaded prediction is not reachable through the
Python bindings. (`*`) KDD Cup 99 mixes continuous and categorical
features.

For a Born-backend microbench (Windows / WebGPU) see
[docs/benchmark-baseline.md](docs/benchmark-baseline.md).

## Limitations

- **LightGBM** — limited set of transformations (sigmoid and softmax only).
- **XGBoost** — limited set of transformations (sigmoid and softmax only);
  floating-point rounding means C-API and `_leaves_` predictions may differ
  by a handful of ULPs.
- **scikit-learn** — no transformation support (output is the raw score from
  `GradientBoostingClassifier.decision_function`); only pickle protocol `0`;
  same float-rounding caveat as XGBoost.

## Roadmap

The end-to-end roadmap (parity with XGBoost 3.3 across training,
inference, serving, and observability) lives in
[演进计划.md](演进计划.md) — **v4.3** lands format sniffing,
`AutoTransform` by default, and a training convenience API. The
executable backlog is in [TODO.md](TODO.md) (P0–T5 are closed).
